content.text:
*"For everything there is a season, and a time for 
every matter under heaven."*{Ecclesiastes 3:1}{A partial order organizes elements by precedence without requiring every pair to be comparable.}{Garrett Birkhoff}

A **partial order** captures the idea of 
ranking or hierarchy.

content.title: Partial Order
content.content:
A relation $R$ on $A$ is a **partial order** if it is:

1. Reflexive: $aRa$ for all $a$

2. Antisymmetric: $aRb$ and $bRa$ imply $a = b$

3. Transitive: $aRb$ and $bRc$ imply $aRc$

The pair $(A, R)$ is called a **poset** (partially ordered set).

content.title: Common partial orders
content.problem:
- $(\R, \leq)$: usual "less than or equal"

- $(\Z^+, \mid)$: divisibility

- $(\mathcal{P}(S), \subseteq)$: set inclusion

- (English words, alphabetical order)

content.title: Comparable
content.content:
In a poset $(A, \preceq)$, elements $a$ and $b$ are **comparable** 
if $a \preceq b$ or $b \preceq a$; otherwise, they are **incomparable**.

content.text:
A **total order** (or linear order) is a partial 
order in which every two elements are comparable.

content.title: Partial vs. total order
content.problem:
$(\R, \leq)$ is a total orderâ€”any two reals are comparable.

$(\{2, 3, 6, 12\}, \mid)$ is only a partial order: 2 and 3 are incomparable 
(neither divides the other).

content.text: A **Hasse diagram** visualizes a finite poset:

items: Draw larger elements higher than smaller ones
Connect $a$ to $b$ if $a \prec b$ and there's nothing between them
Omit loops and implied edges (from transitivity)

content.title: Maximal, Minimal, Greatest, Least
content.content:
In poset $(A, \preceq)$:

- $a$ is **maximal** if no $b \neq a$ satisfies $a \preceq b$

- $a$ is **minimal** if no $b \neq a$ satisfies $b \preceq a$

- $a$ is **greatest** if $b \preceq a$ for all $b$

- $a$ is **least** if $a \preceq b$ for all $b$

content.text: A poset may have multiple maximal elements but at most one greatest element.

marginNotes.text:
Let everyone be subject to the governing 
authorities, for there is no authority except that which God has 
established.
